source: InterNACHI SoP §3.7 — GFCI required in wet locations

visible_defects.defect: Standard duplex receptacle within ~3 ft of kitchen sink, no GFCI

vision_analysis.context_notes: Outlet is on backsplash directly to the right of the sink basin.

input.transcript: Outlet next to the kitchen sink, not GFCI. This needs to be GFCI protected.

expected.rationale: Missing GFCI within 6 ft of a sink is a code violation and shock risk. Clear safety_hazard.

vision_analysis.context_notes: Panel cover removed. Two open knockouts on the lower-right row.

input.transcript: Looking at the panel, there's two open breaker knockouts. Need filler plates here.

expected.rationale: Open knockouts allow finger/foreign-object contact with live bus. SoP explicitly calls this out as a required defect to report.

vision_analysis.context_notes: Three-prong outlet, tester shows hot/neutral reversed.

input.transcript: Outlet in the master bedroom is reverse polarity. Tester is showing hot and neutral swapped.

expected.rationale: Reverse polarity is a wiring defect. Not immediately dangerous like exposed wire, but a shock risk to connected devices.

source: Inspector judgment overrides vision AI when they conflict

vision_analysis.context_notes: Photo angle obscures the test/reset buttons.

input.transcript: This is the bathroom outlet. It tested fine on the GFCI tester — it's GFCI protected upstream from a different outlet, I confirmed it trips.

expected.rationale: Inspector explicitly says it's GFCI-protected via upstream device. Trust the inspector. Severity = info (it's a confirming observation, not a defect).

vision_analysis.primary_subject: hallway ceiling, no smoke detector present

visible_defects.defect: No smoke detector visible in hallway leading to bedrooms

vision_analysis.context_notes: Standard residential hallway, no mounting hole or wiring visible.

input.transcript: Hallway outside the bedrooms — no smoke detector. There's one in the living room and the kitchen, but nothing here. This is a sleeping area, needs one.

expected.rationale: SoP requires reporting absence of smoke detectors. Sleeping-area adjacent hallway is highest-risk location.
